stories.headline: EPA deregulates toxic wastewater limits on coal-fired plants
stories.summary: EPA on Thursday moved to roll back limits on toxic wastewater discharges from coal-fired power plants, part of the administration's broader push to keep aging coal capacity online and ramp up fossil generation.

stories.headline: DOE coal-plant rescue order heads to DC Circuit
stories.summary: A legal challenge to the Energy Department's emergency orders forcing old coal plants to keep running is argued before the D.C. Circuit at 9:30 a.m. Friday, testing the administration's authority to override retirement decisions.

stories.headline: Data center politics arrive in small-town PA
stories.summary: Op-ed argues hyperscale data center siting fights are spreading from Loudoun-style suburbs into rural Pennsylvania, raising land-use and grid-interconnection questions that hit co-op and rural utility territory directly.

stories.headline: Policy leaders urge PA to get serious about renewable energy
stories.summary: Pittsburgh City Paper notes Pennsylvania ranks high on utility costs and low on renewables deployment; state policy experts argue accelerating clean-energy buildout is the answer, with PJM-RTO real-time LMP at $16.97/MWh and load at 82.8 GW today.

stories.headline: Lehigh Valley Dem primaries test progressive vs. party power
stories.summary: Tuesday's Democratic primaries in two Lehigh Valley races — including HD-22 Tiburcio vs. Gerlach — pit graduated-income-tax progressives against incremental moderates, a proxy fight for the party's direction in Pennsylvania.

stories.headline: PA State Police to create new unit for threats against elected officials
stories.summary: PSP is standing up a dedicated unit after the May 6 arrest of Adam Berryhill, 42, of Lebanon County, who allegedly published a 'hit list' targeting 20 Democratic state lawmakers; legislators only learned of the threats this week.

stories.headline: LCV launches $1.2M ad blitz hitting Scott Perry, Rob Bresnahan on energy costs
stories.summary: League of Conservation Voters' $4M campaign against the GOP 'Big Beautiful Bill' opens in Rep. Bill Huizenga's Michigan district, with Reps. Scott Perry (R-Pa.-10) and Juan Ciscomani (R-Ariz.) named as next targets.

stories.headline: Fetterman's vote keeps Iran war powers in Trump's hands
stories.summary: Sen. John Fetterman provided the decisive vote against a war powers resolution that would have directed President Trump to remove U.S. forces from the Iran conflict, the first Senate attempt since the 60-day War Powers Act window closed.

stories.headline: Senate parliamentarian Byrds DHS reconciliation language
stories.summary: MacDonough ruled out large portions of the Homeland Security title — including $2.5B in extra DHS funding and CBP language — forcing Senate Republicans to rewrite ahead of Friday Judiciary arguments over ballroom-security funding.

stories.headline: House T&I closes in on $550-600B highway reauthorization
stories.summary: Chair Sam Graves (R-Mo.) plans to release text as early as Friday for a markup next week; the package likely includes a $250 EV registration fee and would require Ways & Means action on a gas-tax extension by June at earliest.

stories.headline: Senate Banking advances bipartisan crypto Clarity Act
stories.summary: Banking Chair Tim Scott (R-S.C.) advanced the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act in a bipartisan markup Thursday, ruling out bank- and law-enforcement-backed amendments; tax-writing committees are already drafting a follow-on crypto tax package.

stories.headline: Trump returns from Beijing claiming China trade and Hormuz wins
stories.summary: Trump told Hannity that Xi 'strongly' committed not to send military equipment to Iran and pledged to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz; China agreed to buy 200 Boeing jets — fewer than expected — sending Boeing shares down. WTI jumped 3.8% on Hormuz uncertainty.

stories.headline: Pirates 7, Rockies 2: Endy Rodriguez returns with 2 hits, 2 RBI
stories.summary: Pittsburgh beat Colorado 7-2 in Denver on Thursday as Endy Rodriguez had two hits and drove in two runs in his return to the majors; the Pirates (24-20) open a three-game home set vs. the Phillies at PNC Park Friday.

stories.headline: Steelers open OTAs Monday under Mike McCarthy
stories.summary: Pittsburgh's first OTA practice under new head coach Mike McCarthy is Monday, May 18; edge rusher Nick Herbig's snap-share role is the storyline insiders are watching, with Aaron Rodgers locked out under his UFA tender until July training camp.
